question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026, showing a 0.3% monthly increase. Since 0.3% is well below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution, the market should resolve NO with 100% certainty. The current 31% market price appears to reflect outdated information or confusion about what has already been reported.
key_factors: Official BLS data already released showing 0.3% increase
Market closes tomorrow but outcome is already determined
No possibility of revision that could change outcome from NO to YES
risks: Potential for data revision (very unlikely to change from 0.3% to over 0.9%)
Market inefficiency due to information lag
